description: Rules, obligations, permissions, and prohibitions for Loom workflows

must_read_env.rule: MUST read .env file at session startup for DATABASE_URL and configuration
must_read_env.rationale: Prevents connection errors from using wrong database

must_test_before_commit.rationale: Catch bugs early, maintain code quality

must_ask_when_ambiguous.rule: MUST ask clarifying questions when requirements are ambiguous
must_ask_when_ambiguous.rationale: Prevents wasted effort on wrong implementation

must_apply_boy_scout_rule.rule: MUST apply Boy Scout Rule when touching existing code
must_apply_boy_scout_rule.rationale: Leave code cleaner than you found it - fix errors and improve code quality even when not directly related to current task
examples: Fix nearby typos while implementing feature
Extract duplicated code into shared function
Add missing type annotations to adjacent code
Fix unrelated errors discovered during work session
Add proper error handling to fragile code

may_refactor_adjacent_code.rule: MAY refactor code adjacent to changes if applying Boy Scout Rule
may_refactor_adjacent_code.condition: Only if it improves clarity without changing behavior

may_fix_unrelated_errors.rule: MAY fix errors discovered during work that are not related to current task
may_fix_unrelated_errors.condition: Fix should be low-risk and improve overall codebase health
may_fix_unrelated_errors.rationale: Opportunistic improvement leads to higher quality codebase over time

may_defer_work.rule: MAY defer ACs/tasks to future stories with user approval
may_defer_work.condition: Must document reason and target story

must_not_use_psql_without_env.rule: MUST NOT use `psql "${DATABASE_URL}"` without reading .env first
must_not_use_psql_without_env.alternative: Read .env, extract DATABASE_URL, then use it

must_not_commit_untested_code.rule: MUST NOT commit code without testing
must_not_commit_untested_code.rationale: Untested code breaks CI, wastes team time
must_not_commit_untested_code.alternative: Run tests locally, verify passing, then commit

must_not_use_any_type.alternative: Use `unknown` and narrow with type guards, or define proper types

must_not_ignore_discovered_issues.rule: MUST NOT ignore obvious errors or issues discovered while working
must_not_ignore_discovered_issues.rationale: Boy Scout Rule - leaving known issues unfixed degrades codebase
must_not_ignore_discovered_issues.alternative: Fix the issue if low-risk, or document it for future work if complex

story_dod: All non-deferred ACs tested and passing
